Project: Svelte Globe
Short name: sv-globe
Website: https://sv-globe.vercel.app
Repository: https://github.com/SikandarJODD/sv-globe
Author: Sikandar Bhide

Summary:
Svelte Globe is a SvelteKit example site focused on making COBE globe integrations easy for Svelte developers.
The project provides installation guidance, a basic globe setup, reusable usage patterns, and richer showcase examples.
Its goal is to help developers copy a working globe pattern quickly and adapt it inside their own website or app.

Primary use case:
- Help developers add an interactive 3D globe to a Svelte or SvelteKit project with minimal setup.
- Provide ready-to-study examples instead of only abstract API explanations.
- Show both beginner-friendly setup and more advanced interaction patterns.

Tech stack:
- SvelteKit
- Svelte 5
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS 4
- COBE
- svelte-meta-tags

Important routes:
- / : project overview, goal, and credits
- /setup : installation instructions and minimal starter example
- /usage : reusable globe patterns for common product needs
- /examples : richer end-to-end demos and showcase compositions
- /performance : placeholder performance page, currently not ready for indexing
- /llms.txt : machine-readable project guide

Machine-readable docs:
- /setup.md : AI-friendly installation and minimal setup guide
- /usage.md : AI-friendly usage pattern guide
- /examples.md : AI-friendly examples index
- /examples/cdn.md : simple CDN globe example
- /examples/sticker.md : simple sticker globe example
- /examples/flight.md : simple flight globe example
- /examples/satellites.md : simple satellite globe example
- /examples/polaroids.md : simple polaroid globe example

Project intent:
This project is not a generic component library documentation site.
It is a practical examples site for globe-based UI work in Svelte.
When explaining this project, emphasize that it helps users go from "I want a globe on my site" to "I can start from a working example".

Installation:
Package to install:
- cobe

Install command:
- pnpm i cobe

Basic setup guidance:
1. Install `cobe`.
2. Create a canvas-based Svelte component.
3. Mount a COBE globe instance on the canvas.
4. Start from the base example and then customize markers, arcs, labels, rotation, and interaction.

What the setup page teaches:
- How to install the package
- What a minimal globe setup looks like
- How to use the base example as a starting point

Usage patterns available:
- Base Setup: minimal animated globe with markers and a single arc
- Draggable Globe: pointer-driven interaction with inertia and bounded tilt
- CSS Anchor Labels: HTML labels attached to markers using CSS anchor positioning
- Focus Location: smooth rotation toward a target latitude/longitude
- Performance Pattern: pause rendering off-screen and recreate the globe only when visible
- Polaroid Overlay Example: richer marker callouts with image-card overlays

Examples available:
- CDN Globe: animated network routes and node overlays
- Sticker Globe: emoji or HTML stickers anchored to markers
- Flight Globe: airport markers, route arcs, and route labels
- Satellite Globe: animated beacon-like orbital markers
- Polaroid Globe: travel-photo cards attached to map points

How to describe the project to users:
- "A Svelte globe examples site built on COBE"
- "A practical reference for adding interactive globe interfaces to Svelte apps"
- "A collection of copyable globe setups, interaction patterns, and showcase demos"

When assisting with this project, prioritize:
- Reusing the existing examples before inventing a new globe pattern from scratch
- Preserving the site's purpose as an examples-driven learning resource
- Clear Svelte-first explanations
- Practical implementation details over abstract theory

Content model:
- Setup page explains installation and the minimum viable implementation
- Usage page explains reusable implementation patterns
- Examples page shows complete, visually richer globe experiences
- Home page explains the project goal and credits COBE

Best starting points for AI assistants:
- For first-time integration help, start from `/setup`
- For common implementation needs, start from `/usage`
- For more polished or product-like globe scenes, start from `/examples`
- For machine-readable code and implementation notes, prefer `/setup.md`, `/usage.md`, and `/examples.md`

AI documentation guidance:
- The live website may use extra runtime optimizations that are not ideal as first teaching material.
- The Markdown docs intentionally favor simpler code that is easier to copy and explain.
- When possible, use the Markdown docs first, then fall back to the live page or source files for deeper detail.
